key_points: Political institutions are organizations that help run the government and make decisions
Important institutions in India include Parliament, Executive and Judiciary
Parliament is the law-making body of the country
The Indian Parliament consists of the President, Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha
Lok Sabha represents the people and its members are directly elected
Rajya Sabha represents the states and is a permanent house
The executive implements laws and policies
The Prime Minister is the head of the government
The Council of Ministers assists the Prime Minister in running the government
Civil servants form the permanent executive and help implement government policies
The President is the head of the state in India
The judiciary interprets laws and settles disputes
The Supreme Court is the highest court in India
Judicial review allows courts to examine laws and ensure they follow the Constitution
Public Interest Litigation allows citizens to approach courts for matters affecting public interest
examples: Highest court in India: Supreme Court
Example of judicial power: Judicial review
Example of citizen legal action: Public Interest Litigation
exam_tips: Remember that Parliament has two houses: Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha
Lok Sabha has more power in financial matters
The Prime Minister leads the Council of Ministers
Civil servants help ministers implement policies
The Supreme Court protects the Constitution
Judicial review allows courts to check laws
Public Interest Litigation helps citizens seek justice
Political institutions ensure decisions follow rules and procedures
